concerns.text: The plan relies on 'MixSub-LLaMA-3.2' and 'grug-think' to generate synthetic 'latent_delta_magnitude' and 'turn_label' data for a video/audio generation model. These datasets contain text and interaction logs, not audio-visual latent vectors. Mapping text embeddings to 'latent delta magnitudes' for a flow-matching video solver is a fundamental modality mismatch; the synthetic data will not reflect the true statistical properties of the target 'low-information manifolds' in the video latent space, rendering the estimator training and subsequent TOST validation invalid for the research question.

The plan cites 'hotpot_FiD' as a proxy for 'high-dimensional latent vectors to test the FID calculation pipeline'. hotpot_FiD is a question-answering dataset (text), not a video dataset. It cannot be used to compute Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) for video generation, nor can it serve as a proxy for video latent trajectories. The FID metric requires image/video feature extractors (e.g., Inception-V3) applied to visual data, which this dataset does not provide.

The plan proposes using a 'CLIP-based video quality model' as a proxy for MOS. While CLIP is text-image aligned, standard CLIP models are not trained to predict video quality or temporal consistency (MOS) for generative video tasks. Using a text-image model to validate video generation quality (FID/MOS) introduces a severe modality gap. The plan must cite a specific video-quality assessment model (e.g., a pre-trained VQA model or a video-specific CLIP variant) or acknowledge that the proxy metric is unverified for this domain.
